output:
# UCAP and MOSAIC: Content-Conditioned, Budget-Identical Reallocation of Ferret-UI's Anyres Partition for Mobile UI Grounding

This analysis targets the central architectural mechanism of Ferret-UI (You et al., arXiv:2404.05719) - its "any resolution" (anyres) sub-image partition - in the setting of grounded mobile UI understanding (referring plus grounding). It names a representational gap, UCAP (Uniform Content-blind Anyres Partition), and proposes a method, MOSAIC (Monotone Observable-density Sub-image Allocation under Identical-budget Collapse), that generalizes the partition while collapsing exactly back to it by construction.

## 1. Background and Current State of the Art

Multimodal LLMs for grounded UI understanding must both REFER to input regions (point, box, scribble) and GROUND outputs to precise on-screen locations. Ferret-UI [1] is the first MLLM to target this for mobile UIs, built on Ferret [2] and structured around three pillars (architecture, data curation, benchmark) over elementary tasks (widget classification, icon recognition, OCR, find widget/icon/text, widget listing) and advanced tasks (detailed description, perception/interaction conversation, function inference). Its central architectural mechanism is "any resolution" (anyres): because UI screens have elongated aspect ratios and tiny objects (icons, fine text) that a directly resized low-resolution global image loses, Ferret-UI splits each screen into sub-images by aspect ratio (g(a) = 1x2 portrait, 2x1 landscape), encodes each at higher effective resolution, and recombines with the global image.

Prior lines are each partial. The natural-image referring-and-grounding family - Ferret [2], Shikra [3], Kosmos-2 [4] - establishes refer-plus-ground but does not address UI screens or a density-conditioned anyres partition. The UI-specific encoders - Pix2Struct [8], Spotlight [9], CogAgent [10], ILuvUI [27] - operate whole-screen, region-of-interest, or through a fixed high-resolution branch, with a resolution schedule set once rather than conditioned on where information sits. UI-navigation agents - MM-Navigator [22], AppAgent [23], Mobile-Agent [24] - use GPT-4V [14] as a perception engine over whole screenshots, and click-level grounding agents such as SeeClick [25] assume the model can already resolve very small controls. None jointly cover all UI dimensions with referring plus grounding.

The high-resolution tiling line that Ferret-UI's anyres belongs to - LLaVA-1.5/1.6 (LLaVA-NeXT) [6][7], Monkey [15], TextMonkey [16], UReader [17], mPLUG-DocOwl [18] - reaches fine-text fidelity by tiling, but chooses the tiling by IMAGE SHAPE (aspect ratio or a fixed sliding window), not by where information sits. The two lines that DO move resolution adaptively each break a different constraint: visual search V*/SEAL [19] zooms but only with a target query in hand, and token pruning/merging - FastV [20], ToMe [21], PruMerge [26] - moves adaptively only by REDUCING the token budget for compute. Crucially, none of these revisits the UNIFORM, content-blind nature of the anyres partition itself, which this work (the UCAP gap and the MOSAIC fix) targets.

## 2. Key Findings and Analysis

THE GAP - UCAP (Uniform Content-blind Anyres Partition). Ferret-UI's anyres operator P_0 fixes small-UI-element signal loss by splitting each screen into sub-images chosen SOLELY by the global aspect ratio a(S): g(a) = 1x2 for portrait, 2x1 for landscape. P_0 is a FIXED, CONTENT-BLIND SCALAR RULE: it assigns the same spatial-resolution / sub-image budget to every region of the screen, conditioned on one global covariate (aspect ratio) and nothing about WHERE information actually sits. But mobile UI information density is highly NON-UNIFORM WITHIN a single screen - status bars, keyboards, tab bars and toolbars pack many tiny icons and fine OCR text, while content panes are often near-empty. A single aspect-ratio-chosen uniform grid has no degree of freedom that can move resolution toward dense, small-element regions and away from sparse ones; the necessary per-region variation lives in a subspace P_0 cannot reach.

The harm has the required SILENT/SCALES/VANISHES profile. It is SILENT under the standard screen-averaged / pooled referring-plus-grounding accuracy Acc_pool: large easy regions (sparse content panes, big widgets) dominate the average and mask the loss on small dense elements. It SCALES with intra-screen density heterogeneity H(S) (the spread of the per-region density field {d_i}): the more a screen mixes dense and sparse regions, the more a uniform grid mis-allocates. It VANISHES when density is spatially uniform (H(S) = 0, all d_i equal), where the uniform grid is ALREADY optimal allocation and the gap is exactly zero. The residual error therefore CONCENTRATES on small / densely packed targets (tiny tappable icons, fine text) in high-density regions - precisely the interaction-critical elements for any downstream UI agent. The correct measurement is Acc_small (accuracy restricted to elements below size threshold tau), NOT Acc_pool.

THE METHOD - MOSAIC (Monotone Observable-density Sub-image Allocation under Identical-budget Collapse). MOSAIC promotes the fixed content-blind partition P_0 into a STRUCTURED, MONOTONE, BUDGET-CONSTRAINED, hierarchically PARTIALLY-POOLED allocation map pi_theta conditioned on an OBSERVABLE per-region density covariate d - operating PURELY at the deployed anyres encoder layer. It is a DEPLOYED-LAYER-ONLY change: the frozen visual encoder weights phi, the projector psi, and the LLM weights W are UNTOUCHED (no retraining, no new heads). The covariate d is query-free and pre-query: each d_i is the UI-element count from the detection+OCR pipeline Ferret-UI ALREADY runs to build its training data, or a cheap edge/text-saliency pre-pass over the global image. The mechanism estimates the per-region density field, applies hierarchical partial pooling that shrinks each d_i toward the screen mean d_bar, then chooses integer sub-image counts m_i nondecreasing in pooled d_i subject to the FIXED total budget sum_i m_i = B (the exact budget P_0 spends). A single knob theta >= 0 sets allocation strength; theta = 0 yields uniform allocation, i.e. P_0.

THE EXACT-COLLAPSE IDENTITY DEFAULT. At theta = 0, OR under uniform density (all d_i equal, H(S) = 0), pi_theta reduces EXACTLY to P_0: same number of sub-images, same crop boundaries / grid, same effective resolution per sub-image, hence identical encoder input tensors up to floating-point construction. Budget identity sum_i m_i = B holds at EVERY theta, so every Acc comparison is budget-fair. The numeric do-no-harm safety floor is E_collapse := max over screens S of || F_MOSAIC(S; theta=0) - F_P0(S) ||_inf, gated at E_collapse <= 1e-6. This is float-tolerance scoped (identical-grid re-encode; NOT a bitwise claim), budget-identical, and shadow-deployable: turning MOSAIC on at theta = 0 cannot change a single prediction beyond float noise, and CI fails the build on any regression above 1e-6.

EPISTEMIC STATUS. The exact-collapse identity and the budget identity sum_i m_i = B are [provable-by-construction]. The allocator (the density covariate d, the partial-pooling shrinkage, the monotone water-filling integer allocation, and the stratified Acc_small / H(S)-decile metrics) is [designed]. All accuracy lifts are [conjectured]: the sole proven guarantee is the exact-collapse / do-no-harm floor.

## 3. Novel Research Idea (the 5-question proposal)

**[Question 1] - What is the problem?**

Does Ferret-UI's content-blind, aspect-ratio-fixed anyres partition, by allocating an identical spatial-resolution budget to every region of a mobile UI screen, systematically under-resolve the small and densely packed elements in high-density regions in a way that is hidden by screen-averaged referring and grounding accuracy?

**[Question 2] - Why is it interesting and important?**

The harm sits exactly where it hurts. Mobile UIs are density-heterogeneous BY DESIGN: status bars, software keyboards, tab bars and toolbars pack many tiny icons and fine OCR text into a few rows of pixels, while content panes (a message body, a map, a photo) are comparatively sparse. The corpora the field already builds on make this explicit - RICO [13] catalogues 72k Android screens whose chrome is structurally distinct and far denser than their content regions, and Widget Captioning [12] and Screen2Words [11] annotate exactly these small functional elements because they are the units a user acts on. Ferret-UI [1] is built to refer to and ground such elements, and its own anyres mechanism exists precisely because a resized global image loses tiny objects; yet its partition P_0 is chosen solely by aspect ratio a(S) and is therefore content-blind. The resulting loss is SILENT under Acc_pool (big easy regions dominate), SCALES with intra-screen heterogeneity H(S), and VANISHES at H(S) = 0 - concentrating on the small tappable icons and fine text that Acc_small (elements below threshold tau) exposes but Acc_pool hides.

The community-level stakes are high because grounding is a load-bearing primitive and the metric is broken. Grounded UI understanding sits under a fast-growing stack: GPT-4V-driven phone navigation (MM-Navigator [22]), agentic app control (AppAgent [23], Mobile-Agent [24]), and click-level grounding (SeeClick [25]) all assume the model can resolve and localize the very small controls UCAP under-resolves. A screen-averaged metric that hides small-element loss does community-level damage: it certifies systems on Acc_pool while they silently fail on the dense chrome where a single mis-grounded toggle breaks an entire navigation episode. This is a measurement-reform argument with precedent - just as text-rich document/UI MLLMs (Monkey [15], TextMonkey [16], UReader [17], mPLUG-DocOwl [18]) had to introduce high-resolution evaluation to surface fine-text failures, UI grounding needs a density-stratified metric (Acc_small at threshold tau).

The idea also reframes a whole design axis for future research, at zero extra inference cost. The novel object MOSAIC introduces - a density-conditioned, budget-fixed, exactly-collapsing reallocation of a fixed sub-image grid - is not UI-specific: every high-resolution MLLM that tiles an image (LLaVA-NeXT [7], Monkey [15], TextMonkey [16], UReader [17], mPLUG-DocOwl [18]) currently chooses its tiling by image shape, not by where information sits, so the same primitive applies to dense scene text, document pages and charts. Because the budget identity sum_i m_i = B holds at every theta, the gain comes with NO additional tokens and no extra inference cost - a hard requirement for the on-device, latency-bound settings where UI agents (MM-Navigator [22], AppAgent [23], Mobile-Agent [24], SeeClick [25]) and accessibility tools (Spotlight [9], Screen2Words [11], Widget Captioning [12]) run. The deeper knowledge advance is to turn a PASSIVE architectural choice - a fixed, content-blind grid baked into the anyres step (vanilla Ferret-UI [1], the broader LLaVA-1.5/1.6 anyres line [6][7]) - into a CONTROLLABLE, observable-covariate-conditioned allocation with a SAFETY FLOOR, where theta = 0 collapses exactly to P_0 (E_collapse <= 1e-6) [provable-by-construction] and the Acc_small lift is conjectured and empirically testable, never asserted as guaranteed.

**[Question 3] - Why is it hard?**

The target is a content-ADAPTIVE per-region allocation pi_theta over the regions R = {r_i} of a screen S, driven by an observable density field d = {d_i}, that must live INSIDE three constraints that actively fight one another. Each constraint alone is easy; the difficulty is that satisfying any two tends to break the third. (i) TOKEN-IDENTICAL budget: sum_i m_i = B fixed, needed for a fair Acc comparison AND for exact collapse, which rules out the obvious fix of "just add more crops" (that inflates B and destroys both budget-fairness and the collapse identity). (ii) QUERY-FREE: allocation must happen BEFORE any referring/grounding query is seen, so the covariate d must be screen-only - we cannot peek at a target the way V*/SEAL visual search [19] does. (iii) FROZEN STACK: encoder phi, projector psi and LLM W are untouched, so the allocator must emit inputs the EXISTING frozen encoder already handles (same crop/grid family), which is exactly why collapse can be made exact but also forbids any learned-input scheme that drifts off the supported grid set.

Six obstacles follow. O1 COMBINATORIAL INTEGER ALLOCATION: the m_i are integer sub-image counts under the hard equality sum_i m_i = B, an integer partition problem where naive greedy fill can land on a grid the encoder does not support; it must be restricted to the encoder's supported grid family. O2 NOISY LOCAL DENSITY: local d_i from element count or saliency is noisy on a fine lattice, so direct allocation over-drives toward spurious peaks; hierarchical partial pooling shrinking each d_i toward d_bar is required so allocation tracks real, not sampling, heterogeneity. O3 STAYING INSIDE THE FROZEN ENCODER'S GRID FAMILY [provable-by-construction]: every allocated sub-image must remain within the encoder's supported resolution/grid set so no retraining is needed and collapse stays exact. O4 DEFINING AN OBSERVABLE, QUERY-FREE, CHEAP COVARIATE: d must be computable from the screen alone, with no new supervision and no target query (UI-element count from Ferret-UI's own detection+OCR pipeline, or a cheap edge/text-saliency pre-pass). O5 SIMULTANEOUS LIFT AND EXACT COLLAPSE: the method must raise Acc_small on heterogeneous screens AND provably do no harm at default - two objectives that pull against each other, since any reallocation that helps must also be exactly null-able without residue.

O6 - the BINDING obstacle - is METRIC BLINDNESS, and it gates all the others. The harm UCAP inflicts is SILENT under Acc_pool: large easy regions dominate the screen-average and mask the loss on small dense elements, so a naive evaluation on pooled accuracy shows roughly zero effect and WRONGLY REJECTS the method. Before the contribution can even be MEASURED, one must first CONSTRUCT the right stratified metric - Acc_small (elements below size tau), accuracy versus H(S) decile, accuracy versus element-size/density decile. Measurement design, not allocation design, is the rate-limiting step. This is why naive approaches fail: "add more crops" breaks budget identity (i); "learn the allocation end-to-end" retrains or adds heads, breaking the frozen stack (iii) and the exact-collapse construction; "query-guided zoom" (V*/SEAL-style) needs the target query, breaking (ii), and survives only as a non-deployable oracle upper bound; "uniform finer grid at matched budget" spends the same B uniformly and cannot beat P_0 on H(S) > 0 screens (it is the control, not a fix); and "evaluate on Acc_pool" makes a real gain read as roughly zero - failing before any method is even tried.

**[Question 4] - Why hasn't it been solved before?**

The concrete novelty claim is that MOSAIC is, to our knowledge, the first UI/MLLM method whose anyres sub-image allocation JOINTLY satisfies all four clauses: C1 observable query-free per-region density conditioning, C2 fixed-budget partition (token-identical to vanilla anyres), C3 EXACT collapse to vanilla Ferret-UI's fixed grid at theta = 0 (CI-gateable, E_collapse <= 1e-6), and C4 deployed-encoder-layer operation with hierarchical partial pooling (frozen phi/psi/W). It is therefore a STRICT GENERALIZATION of P_0, not a competing black box: P_0 is the theta = 0 special case of pi_theta. Every adjacent line covers AT MOST 2 of C1-C4, because each solved a different problem.

(1) Vanilla Ferret-UI anyres [1] - the collapse target - covers C2 (a fixed sub-image budget B) and C4 (deployed encoder layer), but MISSES C1 (the partition is content-blind, conditioned only on a(S)) and C3 (it IS the floor, not an adaptation, and cannot collapse to itself). (2) LLaVA-1.5/1.6 (LLaVA-NeXT) [6][7] selects from fixed grid configurations by ASPECT RATIO and tiles: C2, C4 only; selection is driven by image shape, not a per-region density field (no C1), and switching grids is a discrete engineering choice, not a theta = 0 identity (no C3). (3) High-res document/UI crop MLLMs - Monkey [15], TextMonkey [16], UReader [17], mPLUG-DocOwl [18] - use fixed sliding-window or shape-adaptive crops: C2, C4 only; they raise resolution UNIFORMLY within a fixed window grid (no per-region density conditioning, no C1) with no exact collapse to a named source partition (no C3). (4) Visual search V*/SEAL [19] and query-guided zoom cover at most C4 but are QUERY-DRIVEN (decisively miss C1), add/expand glimpses rather than reallocating a fixed B (miss C2), and never collapse to a fixed grid (miss C3). (5) Token pruning/merging - FastV [20], ToMe [21], PruMerge [26] - cover at most C4 but REDUCE the budget rather than reallocating a fixed B (decisively miss C2), are driven by attention/feature similarity not an observable density covariate (miss C1), and have no collapse to vanilla anyres (miss C3). (6) UI-specific and grounding models - CogAgent [10] (fixed high-res cross-attention branch), Spotlight [9] and Pix2Struct [8] (whole-screen / region encoders), and the natural-image grounding line Ferret [2], Shikra [3], Kosmos-2 [4] - cover at most C4, with fixed or trained-in resolution paths and no density conditioning, no fixed-budget reallocation, and no collapse.

The barrier is that the field treated anyres partitioning as an ASPECT-RATIO ENGINEERING DETAIL set once by image shape (Ferret-UI [1], LLaVA-NeXT [7], Monkey [15]) and evaluated progress on POOLED accuracy that hid the small-element loss (the SILENT property), so there was no metric pressure to revisit the grid; the two adaptive lines each broke a constraint MOSAIC must keep (visual search [19] by being query-driven and budget-elastic, token pruning [20][21][26] by reducing the budget). In honesty, the strongest near-miss is the dense-text crop line [15][16][17][18] - they DO raise effective resolution for fine text, and we do NOT claim to discover that small text needs more pixels; the boundary is that they raise resolution UNIFORMLY within a fixed crop/window grid, whereas MOSAIC conditions placement on an observable per-region density field d, holds B fixed by reallocation, and collapses EXACTLY to the source grid at theta = 0. The clean novelty sentence: no prior UI/MLLM line combines C1 (observable query-free density conditioning) AND C2 (fixed-budget partition) AND C3 (exact collapse to vanilla anyres) AND C4 (deployed-encoder-layer with partial pooling); MOSAIC occupies the uncovered joint.

**[Question 5] - What are the key components of my approach and results?**

METHOD PIPELINE (MOSAIC). Given screen S with aspect ratio a(S) and the frozen stack (phi, psi, W): STEP 1 - density field estimation [designed]: lay a base lattice R = {r_i}, i = 1..K over S (K chosen so each cell maps to a candidate sub-image of the encoder's supported grid family) and compute the observable covariate d_i = UI-element count in r_i from Ferret-UI's own detection+OCR pipeline, OR edge/text-saliency energy from a cheap pre-pass, d_i >= 0, query-free and pre-query. STEP 2 - hierarchical partial pooling [designed]: shrink each d_i toward the screen mean, d_i_pooled = d_bar + lambda(theta) * (d_i - d_bar) with 0 <= lambda(theta) <= 1, a James-Stein / random-effects shrinkage weight; lambda(0) = 0 (full pooling, all cells equal, uniform) and lambda grows with theta toward trusting local d (handles O2). STEP 3 - monotone budget-constrained integer allocation [designed]: choose m_i with (M1) m_i nondecreasing in d_i_pooled, (M2) sum_i m_i = B exactly, (M3) every (r_i, m_i) a member of the encoder's SUPPORTED grid family; pi_theta : d -> {m_i}. STEP 4 - encode and combine with the frozen stack [provable, C4]: encode allocated sub-images plus the resized global image exactly as Ferret-UI does with frozen phi, project with frozen psi, pass to frozen W; no new weights. The knob theta >= 0 jointly controls the pooling weight lambda(theta) and how far m_i may depart from uniform; theta = 0 yields uniform allocation, i.e. P_0.

EXACT-COLLAPSE CONSTRUCTION AND CI GATE [provable-by-construction, C3]. At theta = 0, lambda(0) = 0 so d_i_pooled = d_bar for all i; a uniform field has H(S) = 0, so the monotone allocator is indifferent across cells and falls back to g(a(S)) = 1x2 portrait / 2x1 landscape, i.e. pi_0 = P_0 - same sub-image count, same crop boundaries, same effective resolution, hence identical encoder input tensors up to float construction. The same holds for any theta whenever H(S) = 0. Budget identity sum_i m_i = B holds at EVERY theta (M2), so every Acc comparison is budget-fair. The CI safety floor asserts E_collapse := max over screens S of || F_MOSAIC(S; theta=0) - F_P0(S) ||_inf <= 1e-6 (float tolerance, not bitwise); any regression above 1e-6 fails the build, making MOSAIC shadow-deployable.

TWO ALLOCATOR INSTANTIATIONS. (a) Robust floor - monotone water-filling / proportional-to-pooled-density integer allocator [designed]: target a real-valued allocation proportional to d_i_pooled^theta normalized to sum B, then round to the nearest member of the encoder's supported grid family by integer water-filling (repeatedly hand the next sub-image unit to the region with the largest deficit until sum_i m_i = B), never leaving the supported set; deterministic, O(K log K), provably monotone and budget-exact, exactly P_0 at theta = 0, learning-free. (b) Optional learned-but-frozen-stack scorer [designed, ablation only]: a tiny scorer s(r_i) trained ONLY on the density covariate d (not the task, not queries) fed through the SAME water-filling rounding so (M1)-(M3) and exact collapse still hold; the frozen stack is never touched, and (a) remains the reported robust floor.

DATASETS (REAL). Ferret-UI's own elementary and advanced task suite; RICO [13] (view hierarchy gives element boxes -> covariate d and size strata tau); Screen2Words [11] (screen summarization / text density); Widget Captioning [12] (small-element referring targets); mobile UI element detection / RICO-SCA (element boxes for d and Acc_small); AITW (Android in the Wild) and/or SeeClick [25] grounding (interaction-critical targets); ScreenSpot from SeeClick (grounding localization). Element counts/boxes supply BOTH the query-free covariate d AND the size threshold tau (bbox-area percentile). Optional transfer check: DocVQA / ChartQA for dense document/chart text.

METRICS. PRIMARY: Acc_small - referring + grounding accuracy on elements below threshold tau, where UCAP harm concentrates and which O6 forces us to construct. STRATIFIED (the O6-required views): accuracy by H(S) decile, by element-size decile, by density decile. GROUNDING: IoU, center-in-box, accuracy@IoU. FINE TEXT: OCR exact-match on small text. DO-NO-HARM: Acc_pool no-regression (within +/- epsilon). BUDGET: token-budget overhead = 0 percent (sum_i m_i = B at every theta). SAFETY GATE: E_collapse <= 1e-6 in CI.

BASELINES. B1 resized global image only (low-res floor); B2 vanilla Ferret-UI anyres (the exact-collapse target P_0); B3 LLaVA-NeXT dynamic high-res (external SOTA); B4 uniform finer grid at MATCHED budget B (isolates "more uniform crops" from density-conditioned reallocation); B5 query-guided / oracle zoom (non-deployable UPPER BOUND showing headroom); B6 random / anti-density allocation (PLACEBO; must NOT beat B2); B7 MOSAIC (ours). ABLATIONS: theta sweep (collapse at 0, lift then saturation); density-covariate source (element count vs saliency); partial-pooling strength lambda(theta); with vs without the budget constraint; allocator (a) vs (b).

FALSIFIABILITY TRIPLES. T1 UNIFORM-DENSITY NULL: on synthetic uniform screens (H(S) = 0), MOSAIC MUST equal B2 (no gain) - confirms exact collapse / no spurious gain [provable at theta = 0; empirical for theta > 0 at H(S) = 0]. T2 MONOTONE-DOSE: gain on Acc_small MUST increase with H(S) and with theta up to saturation; a flat or decreasing dose-response falsifies the mechanism [conjectured]. T3 DENSITY-SCRAMBLE / ANTI-DENSITY: permute or reverse d so budget flows to SPARSE regions and the gain MUST disappear or reverse; if a scrambled field still helps, the density signal is not causal [conjectured]. HYPOTHESES (all lifts hedged): H1 Acc_small improves by +3..+8 points on top-H(S)-decile screens at best theta [conjectured]; H2 Acc_pool stays within +/- 0.5 points of B2 [conjectured for theta > 0; provable at theta = 0]; H3 token-budget overhead = 0 percent [provable-by-construction]; H4 E_collapse <= 1e-6 at theta = 0 across all screens [provable-by-construction]; H5 the Acc_small gain is monotone-nondecreasing in H(S) and rises with theta to saturation [conjectured]; H6 placebo B6 is indistinguishable from B2 while B7 > B2 and B7 < B5 oracle (headroom remains) [conjectured]. The sole proven guarantee is the exact-collapse / do-no-harm floor (C3); all accuracy lifts are conjectured and empirically testable.

## 4. References and Evidence

[1] You, K., Zhang, H., Schoop, E., Weers, F., Swearngin, A., Nichols, J., Yang, Y., and Gan, Z. (2024). "Ferret-UI: Grounded Mobile UI Understanding with Multimodal LLMs." arXiv:2404.05719. (Source paper; anyres partition = UCAP / P_0, the collapse target.)

[2] You, H., Zhang, H., Gan, Z., Du, X., Zhang, B., Wang, Z., Cao, L., Chang, S.-F., and Yang, Y. (2023). "Ferret: Refer and Ground Anything Anywhere at Any Granularity." arXiv:2310.07704. (Backbone of Ferret-UI; natural-image refer+ground.)

[3] Chen, K., Zhang, Z., Zeng, W., Zhang, R., Zhu, F., and Zhao, R. (2023). "Shikra: Unleashing Multimodal LLM's Referential Dialogue Magic." arXiv:2306.15195. (Natural-image grounding; no UI anyres.)

[4] Peng, Z., Wang, W., Dong, L., Hao, Y., Huang, S., Ma, S., and Wei, F. (2023). "Kosmos-2: Grounding Multimodal Large Language Models to the World." arXiv:2306.14824. (Natural-image grounding.)

[5] Liu, H., Li, C., Wu, Q., and Lee, Y. J. (2023). "Visual Instruction Tuning (LLaVA)." NeurIPS 2023. arXiv:2304.08485. (Instruction-tuned MLLM baseline.)

[6] Liu, H., Li, C., Li, Y., and Lee, Y. J. (2023). "Improved Baselines with Visual Instruction Tuning (LLaVA-1.5)." arXiv:2310.03744.

[7] Liu, H., Li, C., Li, Y., Li, B., Zhang, Y., Shen, S., and Lee, Y. J. (2024). "LLaVA-NeXT: Improved Reasoning, OCR, and World Knowledge (LLaVA-1.6)." Project release, 2024. (Dynamic high-resolution anyres by aspect ratio; near-miss covering C2, C4.)

[8] Lee, K., Joshi, M., Turc, I., Hu, H., Liu, F., Eisenschlos, J., Khandelwal, U., Shaw, P., Chang, M.-W., and Toutanova, K. (2023). "Pix2Struct: Screenshot Parsing as Pretraining for Visual Language Understanding." ICML 2023. arXiv:2210.03347.

[9] Li, G. and Li, Y. (2023). "Spotlight: Mobile UI Understanding using Vision-Language Models with a Focus." ICLR 2023. arXiv:2209.14927.

[10] Hong, W., Wang, W., Lv, Q., Xu, J., Yu, W., Ji, J., Wang, Y., Wang, Z., Dong, Y., Ding, M., and Tang, J. (2023). "CogAgent: A Visual Language Model for GUI Agents." arXiv:2312.08914. (Fixed high-resolution cross-attention branch; near-miss covering at most C4.)

[11] Wang, B., Li, G., Zhou, X., Chen, Z., Grossman, T., and Li, Y. (2021). "Screen2Words: Automatic Mobile UI Summarization with Multimodal Learning." UIST 2021. arXiv:2108.03353. (Mobile-UI data source; reusable for evaluation.)

[12] Li, Y., Li, G., He, L., Zheng, J., Li, H., and Guan, Z. (2020). "Widget Captioning: Generating Natural Language Description for Mobile User Interface Elements." EMNLP 2020. arXiv:2010.04295. (Small-element annotations; density covariate / evaluation source.)

[13] Deka, B., Huang, Z., Franzen, C., Hibschman, J., Afergan, D., Li, Y., Nichols, J., and Kumar, R. (2017). "Rico: A Mobile App Dataset for Building Data-Driven Design Applications." UIST 2017. (72k-screen mobile-UI dataset; density covariate / evaluation source.)

[14] OpenAI (2023). "GPT-4V(ision) System Card." OpenAI technical report, 2023. (Used as the perception engine by UI-navigation agents.)

[15] Li, Z., Yang, B., Liu, Q., Ma, Z., Zhang, S., Yang, J., Sun, Y., Liu, Y., and Bai, X. (2023). "Monkey: Image Resolution and Text Label Are Important Things for Large Multi-modal Models." arXiv:2311.06607. (Fixed sliding-window high-res crops; near-miss covering C2, C4.)

[16] Liu, Y., Yang, B., Liu, Q., Li, Z., Ma, Z., Zhang, S., and Bai, X. (2024). "TextMonkey: An OCR-Free Large Multimodal Model for Understanding Document." arXiv:2403.04473. (Shape-adaptive high-res crops; near-miss covering C2, C4.)

[17] Ye, J., Hu, A., Xu, H., Ye, Q., Yan, M., Dan, Y., Zhao, C., Xu, G., Li, C., Tian, J., et al. (2023). "UReader: Universal OCR-free Visually-situated Language Understanding with Multimodal Large Language Model." Findings of EMNLP 2023. arXiv:2310.05126. (Shape-adaptive cropping; near-miss C2, C4.)
